title: Stable Subarray Reversal Index Validation with Incorrect Shifts
subCategory: Array Index Shift Detection and Validation

statement.markdown: A stable subarray is a subarray whose elements are in non-decreasing order. The function `solve` has to determine whether all subarrays from 0 to index 1 and index 2 to n-1 are stable, where the subarrays from index 1 to 2 and index i+1 to i+2 are shifted by one element to the left compared to their original positions. The function should return 1 if all conditions are met and 0 otherwise.
statement.inputFormat: An array of integers with a size n (n >= 3).
statement.outputFormat: 1 if all conditions are met and 0 otherwise.
statement.notes: Subarrays with one element are also considered stable.

hints.text: First hint text, use 2 pointers to check stability of subarrays.
